marketState: neutral - price sits at fair value with strong weather demand pulling bullish against a bearish storage surplus and a still-intact medium-term downtrend
summary: Front-month NG is trading almost exactly at model fair value ($2.87 vs $2.917, spread -$0.047, status FAIR), so there's no fair-value edge right now. Weather is the standout bullish input: 1-7D and 8-16D demand are +18.4% and +15.3% vs the 5-year norm with rangePos near the top of its band (0.79/0.87) and rising (+12.6 in 24h), consistent with strong cooling-season load. That's offset by a bearish storage print (+184 Bcf vs 5y, a large +72 Bcf bearish surprise vs forecast) and daily/weekly technicals that remain in a bearish trend with a 50<200 death cross, even as shorter intraday frames look oversold (RSI 27-35, price below the 15m lower Bollinger band). Seasonality is unreliable this week (59pp 10-90 spread) and shows only a 38% chance of higher prices 3-4 weeks out with a -1.4% typical move, so it's a weak tailwind at best; COT managed-money is net short but only at the 35th percentile, meaning positioning isn't crowded either way. Net-net: mixed evidence with no single dominant edge, favoring patience over aggressive positioning in either direction.

longTerm.rationale: Fair value drifts higher over the next 21 days ($2.917 -> $3.020) and weather demand is strongly above normal, both mildly bullish, but that's countered by a bearish storage surplus (+184 Bcf vs 5y, +72 Bcf bearish build surprise) and low-reliability seasonality showing only a 38% chance of higher prices 3-4 weeks out with a -1.4% typical move. The evidence doesn't line up cleanly enough for a swing trade yet.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) price to dip toward the lower fair-value band (~$2.55-$2.65) while weather demand stays elevated, favoring LONG into the seasonally strong Sep window, or (b) storage surprises flip from bearish builds to draws and probHigher (3-4wk) climbs above ~55%, or (c) a confirmed daily trend flip (EMA50 back above EMA200, MACD turning positive) to support entering long toward fair value.

shortTerm.rationale: Intraday RSI is oversold (27-35) and 15m price sits below the lower Bollinger band, hinting at a mean-reversion bounce, and near-term weather demand is strongly bullish (+18.4% vs 5y). But price is already at fair value (spread only -$0.047, status FAIR) so there's no discount to buy, and 1h/4h/1d/1w trends are all bearish with a 50<200 death cross and mostly negative MACD histograms, meaning the larger trend hasn't turned. Futures curve data is missing (null), removing a useful confirming signal.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for confirmation of a bounce: MACD histogram turning positive on 1h/4h with price reclaiming the 1h EMA50, or a pullback toward $2.65-$2.70 (near the lower fair-value band) that holds while weather demand stays elevated. A daily close below $2.60 would instead favor a short toward the storage-surplus/bearish-technical case.
